steps.title: Identify the core claim
steps.content: Núnez claims that the pattern of using opposite directions along an axis to distinguish past from future is not universal. To support this, we need evidence that Yupno speakers do not use opposite directions for temporal concepts.

steps.title: Locate evidence against opposite directions
steps.content:
Choice B — Future-oriented gestures used by Yupno speakers do not, on average, point in the opposite linear direction of past-oriented gestures. states directly that Yupno future and past gestures do not point in opposite directions—proving they don't follow the universal pattern.

The other three choices address hand choice, grammatical overlap, or gesture contexts, but don't show whether opposite directions distinguish past and future.

That matches Choice B — Future-oriented gestures used by Yupno speakers do not, on average, point in the opposite linear direction of past-oriented gestures..
